hypothesis: If lattice-structure property data are organized as a low-rank tensor and only sparse or convenience-biased samples (e.g., ~100/270, including exponentially biased selections) are used for training, then tensor completion models (CPD-S/NeAT and their ensemble) will achieve higher test R² for predicting Young’s modulus and mass-normalized modulus than Gaussian Processes, XGBoost, or MLPs because they exploit multiway low-rank correlations across design-parameter modes.
